name: Barbell Curl
resistance_profile: Heavy bilateral elbow flexion; the classic biceps mass builder.
execution_steps: Stand tall holding a barbell with an underhand (palms-up) grip, hands about shoulder-width, arms hanging straight.
Pin your elbows to your sides and brace your core so the torso stays still.
Curl the bar up toward your shoulders by bending only at the elbows.
Squeeze the biceps at the top without letting the elbows drift forward.
Lower the bar under control back to a full arm extension.
good_when: You want a simple, heavy bilateral lift to build overall biceps mass.
You can add small load increments with a standard or EZ bar for steady progression.
bad_when: A fixed straight-bar grip irritates your wrists or elbows (use an EZ-bar or dumbbells).
You specifically want to load the stretched position (use an incline or preacher curl).
